trait.name: Keen Sight and Smell
entries: The Nurikabe has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight or smell.

trait.name: Extendable
entries: The Nurikabe will extend its body up to 60 ft. extra in order to stop people from passing through.

trait.name: Magically Immobile
entries: The Nurikabe cannot be moved in any way whatsoever, not even by magical means.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Nurikabe makes two attacks with its claws.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) slashing damage.
If you are looking to populate your adventures with more
monsters and creatures from the world that Kelfecil's Tales
takes place in, then make sure you check out the free
supplement Of Starlight and Void supplement.

trait.name: Shapechanger
entries: The kitsune can use its action to polymorph into a Small or Medium humanoid, or back into its true form. Its statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn’t transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

spellcasting.name: Innate Spellcasting
headerEntries: The kitsune’s innate spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save {@dc 12}, +4 to hit with spell attacks). The kitsune can innately cast the following spells, requiring no material components:

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Kitsune makes two attacks with its claws.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d8 + 9}) slashing damage.

action.name: Kitsune's Juxtapose
entries: The Kitsune can take a bonus action to multiply itself by the number of its tails. The extra copies of itself have 1HP and also make attacks but can only do maximum 1 slashing damage. The images all attack at the same time making it impossible for the target to know which one is the real one.
Kobold Press Credit

trait.name: Air Form (Wintry Swirl Form Only)
entries: The kamaitachi can enter a hostile creature’s space and stop there. It can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Flyby (Wintry Swirl Form Only)
entries: The kamaitachi doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks when it flies out of an enemy’s reach.

trait.name: Concealing Winds (True Form Only)
entries: As a bonus action, the kamaitachi sheathes itself in blowing winds, causing attack rolls against it to have disadvantage. The kamaitachi can use this trait only if it is amidst blowing winds.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The kamaitachi has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Shapechanger
entries: The kamaitachi can use its action to polymorph into a swirl of wintry weather. It can revert back to its true form as a bonus action. Its statistics are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn’t transformed. It reverts to its true form when it dies. While a wintry swirl, it has a flying speed of 40 feet, immunity to the {@condition grappled}, {@condition petrified}, {@condition prone}, and {@condition restrained} conditions, and resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The kamaitachi makes two sickle paw attacks.

action.name: Sickle Paw (True Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d6 + 4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Wintry Assault (Wintry Swirl Form Only)
entries: Each creature in the kamaitachi’s space must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) slashing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) cold damage and is {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn. On a success, it takes half the damage and isn’t {@condition blinded}.
Despite having bony sickles for paws, this large weasel moves adroitly as snow and ice whip around it.

{@bold Related to Wind Weasels}. Kamaitachi derive from a family group of wind weasels that migrated and acclimated to far northern forests. As a result, they lost their connection to the fey, shrinking and devolving into more natural beasts. Their claws fused to become one large, sickle-like claw on each paw. They retain the wind weasels’ jealousy of humanoids and aggressively attack intruders.

{@bold Stinging Winds}. Like their fey cousins, the kamaitachi can turn into a swirl of wind, but they incorporate ice and snow instead of leaves, chilling their victims. Even in their weasel forms, they surround themselves with wintry precipitation that obscures them from sight.

{@bold Summer Migrations}. Kamaitachi thrive for most of the year in arctic forests. Prior to summer, they migrate closer to the pole to stay in contact with frozen water, most of which disappears in their forests during the warmer weeks. Once snows begin to fall again, they return to their forests and relentlessly drive out creatures that moved in during their absence
Kobold Press Credit

trait.name: Second Wind
entries: Nakata Kame has a limited well of stamina that he can draw on to protect himself from harm. On his turn, you can use a Bonus Action to regain {@dice 2d10} Hit Points.

trait.name: Umi's Light
entries: Nakata Kame can charge a strike with the moon's energy. His weapon does {@damage 2d10} radiant damage on top of its standard damage.

trait.name: Moon Step
entries: Nakata Kame may use his bonus action to take the Disengage action. Moreover, speed is increased by 5 ft. under the moon's light.

trait.name: Great Weapon Fighting
entries: When Nakata Kame rolls a 1 or 2 on a damage die for an Attack he makes with a melee weapon that he is wielding with two hands, he can reroll the die and must use the new roll, even if the new roll is a 1 or a 2.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Nakata Kame is able to strike three times with his blade while still moving in between each strike.

action.name: Haya
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 1d8 + 10}) slashing damage.
